books.title: Sophie's Rebellion
books.author: Beverley Boissery
books.rationale: Sophie's Rebellion is a fictional account of one American girl's experience of the 1837-1838 Rebellions, as she is held prisoner alongside her British travelling companions and befriends a young rebel named Luc. Through Sophie's eyes, the reader is able to see multiple perspectives of this conflict and is introduced to a number of notable historical figures. A trained historian, Beverley Boissery creates an authentically detailed account of Upper and Lower Canada in the mid-nineteenth century.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 9-10
Canadian History: Grades 7-8

books.title: Early Voices
books.author: Mary Alice Downie
books.rationale: This collection of writings by 29 different women presents a kaleidoscope image of Canada's history. The wide range of individual voices, backgrounds, and histories provided by these primary documents complement the other historical non-fiction and fiction read during the year.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 11-12
Canadian History: Grades 9-10

books.title: The Underground Railroad: Next Stop, Toronto!
books.author: Adrienne Shadd, Afua Cooper, & Karolyn Smardz Frost
books.rationale: The Underground Railroad: Next Stop, Toronto! is a detailed account of Black history in Canada focusing on the stories of those who made the dangerous journey north to freedom. Using a wide variety of primary sources, including archival documents, newspaper articles, letters, photographs, maps, archeological artifacts, and more, this book recounts what happened to those freedom-seekers once they arrived safely and settled. Through this lens, readers are also introduced to what life was like in the city of Toronto in the nineteenth century. The presence and contribution of Black people who made their homes in Toronto contributed significantly to the history and development of the city.

books.title: Red Wolf
books.author: Jennifer Dance
books.rationale: A harrowing and heartbreaking story of the impact of the Indian Act of 1876 on Indigenous families and communities through one child's experience of the Indian Residential School system. This novel is beautifully written and rich in historical detail, presenting an honest introduction to a dark chapter of Canada’s history.

books.title: Vanished Beyond the Map
books.author: Adam Shoalts
books.rationale: Vanished Beyond the Map unfolds like a mystery novel: after first stumbling upon the name Hubert Darrell in an old encyclopedia, Adam Shoalts sets off on a journey through the arctic and the archives to discover as much as he can about this nearly-forgotten explorer, mapmaker and guide who disappeared in 1910. This book is remarkable for showing readers what is involved in trying to solve a historical mystery: where do you find clues? what kinds of questions do you ask? how much can we really know about the past? But it is also a book which situates Darrell within the wider world of geographers and explorers in the late-nineteenth century, who were attempting to complete the map of the world by charting the unknown lands of the arctic and antarctic.

books.title: The Charlottetown Conference and the Birth of Confederation
books.author: Deirdre Kessler
books.rationale: The Charlottetown Conference and the Birth of Confederation is a detailed, engagingly-written account of the historical factors, issues, political players, and conferences which eventually led to Canada's Confederation in 1867. Attention is paid to the motives and pressures urging British North America towards unity, as well as the specific interests and context of each of the colonies before Confederation. The book brings this historical period to life with numerous photographs, paintings, archival documents, and other primary sources.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 7-8
Canadian History: Grades 9-10

books.title: A Little Book for New Historians
books.author: Robert Tracy McKenzie
books.rationale: Robert Tracy McKenzie offers a concise, clear, and beautifully-written introduction to the study of history.  Loaded with concrete examples and insightful principles, this primer shows how the study of history, faithfully pursued, can shape your heart as well as your mind. Videos of the author discussing his book are found in the lesson plans.

books.title: Erebus: The Story of a Ship
books.author: Michael Palin
books.rationale: Erebus is a book about exploration and mystery. Palin takes the student into two of the most harrowing exploration trips ever recorded, the last trip being unsuccessful and leading to a mystery that still puzzles modern-day minds. By reading Erebus, students will get an account of what is known about the first ever explorations into Antarctica, the most isolated continent in the world.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grade 9
World History: Grades 10-12

books.title: Blood and Iron: The Rise and Fall of the German Empire
books.author: Katja Hoyer
books.rationale: Katja Hoyer is a German-British historian and expert in the field of Germany’s history. She brings that expertise to the pages of her book which engages with the people, policies, and places that helped build the German Empire and ultimately led to its downfall. This book offers the student the history of a nation whose national pride led to two World Wars.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grades 10-12
World History: Grade 9

books.title: A Short History of Ancient Rome
books.author: Pascal Hughes
books.rationale: This book charts the dramatic twists and turns of the Roman kingdom, the republic, the empire and its decline. Pascal Hughes writes the history in a popular form that is engaging and easy to understand. Students will walk away from the reading with a deep understanding of one of the most powerful nations in the world.

books.title: The Witness of Early Christian Women: Mothers of the Church
books.author: Mike Aquilina
books.rationale: Source documents/writings alongside short biographies of women and their impact in the early church.
